rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVELYN JUNG WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/8/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service has been filed. (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (Why account when the executor is also the trustee of the devisee trust?) (3) Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. (4) All the funds exceeding the FDIC insured amount are held at one bank. (5) The detailed schedules supporting an accounting must show the date, payee or source, description, and amount. (6) Schedule E does not make sense. (7) The petition does not disclose the fees for the guardian ad litem, and no supplement has been filed. (8) Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. (9) Some of the attorney costs are rather vague and lack sufficient description (of what was filed or sent by messenger, etc.) for the Court to determine if they were all reasonable. (10) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/25/26. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY B. PORTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/11/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 (Notice of Hearing), on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (2) The Notice of Hearing must have ATTACHED TO IT the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) If notice is served electronically, the party who agrees to accept electronic service must affirmatively consent. [CRC 2.251(b).] (4) If notice is served electronically, the proof of electronic service must have attached to it the Notice of Hearing (DE-120) with the remote appearance instructions attached that was served. (5) A petitioner may not serve notice of his own petition or amended petition. (6) Has the original will not been lodged yet, as required by Probate Code Section 8200? (7) Proof of Holographic Instrument (mandatory Judicial Council form DE-135) must be filed. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/25/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Will the nominated co-executors, the children of the decedent and the petitioner, waive notice and consent?)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DERRICK YANO-NAKANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/30/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been FILED, showing that proper notice, with the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to it, was served. (2) Various unfiled papers were dropped off. They were not all courtesy copies (copies of endorsed FILED paper), and one was another (unfiled) petition (and the "petition" was not properly verified). A SUPPLEMENT to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address the issues with the petition on file. Waivers of bond (which should be completely executed by each party waiving bond) may be attached to the supplement or filed separately. (A decedent's parents and siblings are NOT heirs if spouse and children survived.) Proof of service of notice with a printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely must be filed. Courtesy copies of the supplement and proof of service and any other papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 3/16/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF BRIAN WING REZOWALLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although nothing has been filed to address the issues (e-mailed to petitioners in pro per on 12/01/2025). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
